notes: Official Origin product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
You managed your finances exceptionally well in May, Priya, finishing the month with a total spend of $9,256 and a substantial net surplus of $9,394. This surplus represents a 50.37% savings rate from your $18,650 in total income, which is an outstanding achievement that significantly strengthens your financial position.
Spending by category
Your spending was concentrated in a few key areas, with fixed costs for housing and childcare representing the majority of your monthly expenses:
Household: $4,092 (44% of total spend) — this was primarily driven by your $3,850 Rocket Mortgage payment for your Denver home.
Childcare & education: $1,870 (20% of total spend) — your main expense here was the $1,650 payment to Bright Horizons Daycare, plus a backup care session.
Groceries: $1,023 (11% of total spend) — this includes $736 across two trips to Costco and $286 at King Soopers.
Auto loan: $485 — your monthly Toyota Financial payment.
HSA contribution: $400 — your monthly contribution to HealthEquity.
Financial: $372 — reflecting your combined State Farm home and auto insurance premiums.
Insights and progress
Outside of your major fixed costs, you maintained disciplined spending in variable categories. For instance, your drinks & dining expenses totaled only $164 for the month across visits to Blue Pan Pizza and Snooze AM Eatery. Additionally, your consistent $300 contribution to your Colorado 529 CollegeInvest account shows great commitment to your long-term family goals.
This high savings rate for May provides a strong cushion for your cash flow and accelerates your progress toward future wealth accumulation. By keeping your variable spending low while managing your large fixed obligations, you are positioning your household for remarkable financial stability.
